page: Amazon Web Services c7i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz and includes a 105 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance has no local storage and no GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the c7i.4xlarge in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1741.0 and a multi-core score of 10360.0. In text processing, it demonstrated strong performance with a Geekbench score of 2321.0, placing it in the top 25% of tested systems. However, its PassMark memory score of 1757.31 is classified as weak, falling into the bottom 25%, which highlights a potential bottleneck for memory-intensive operations despite the 5 GHz DDR5 specification. Large block memory read bandwidth was measured at 83.57 GB/sec, and write bandwidth at 57.73 GB/sec.

The Intel Xeon CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for integer and tile operations, which assist in cryptographic and vector calculations. In LLM inference tests using a 135M model, the instance processed prompts at 1490.97 tokens/second and generated text at 256.18 tokens/second. For a medium 7B model, prompt processing reached 97.0 tokens/second with text generation at 11.18 tokens/second. Given its compute-to-memory ratio and lack of local storage, this instance is configured for CPU-bound workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics.
description: The Amazon Web Services c7i.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.2 GHz and 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying on network-attached storage with a 6 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate average performance across most metrics, including a Geekbench single-core score of 1741.0 and multi-core score of 10360.0. It shows strong text processing capabilities but exhibits a weak PassMark memory score of 1757.31. Supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions, it is suited for compute-heavy workloads, web serving, and moderate machine learning inference, though memory-bound tasks may experience tradeoffs due to lower relative memory performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for CPU-bound workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.4xlarge is an Intel-based compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 6 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for CPU-intensive workloads and moderate vector calculations.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 3.2 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector math
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for web serving, batch processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference
